title: Local Network Compounding

content.statement: Repetition within a network creates exponential value.
content.question: What mechanic in your business makes each use more valuable than the last, for the same user?
content.equation: LNC = Repeat Usage × Referral × Partner Retention × Frequency
sop: Identify one specific mechanic (data, community, saved preferences, referral rewards) that could make repeat use more valuable than first use.
Rate Repeat Usage 1-10, how often the same customer comes back (see calculation, page 6).
Rate Referral 1-10, how often customers bring in others (see calculation, page 6).
Rate Partner Retention 1-10, how long your supply-side or partners stay active (see calculation, page 6).
Rate Frequency 1-10, how often interactions happen overall (see calculation, page 6), then invest specifically in whichever factor scores lowest.
content.explainer: Network effects live in repeat interaction. One-shot products don't compound; habitual ones do.
questions: What mechanic in your business makes each use more valuable than the last, for the same user?
Does using your product once make someone more likely to use it again, or does every visit start from zero?
If a customer referred a friend, would that friend's presence actually make the original customer's experience better?

definitions.def: When a product becomes more valuable as more people use it.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Repeat Usage 7, Referral 6, Partner Retention 8, Frequency 7. 7 times 6 times 8 times 7 equals 2,352. Higher and rising means your product gets more valuable the more it's used, real compounding. Lower means every use is basically a one-off, and you're stuck re-earning each customer from scratch.
content.closing_statement: Real network effects live inside repeat use, not signups.

caption:
Principle Nº 77: Local Network Compounding

Repetition within a network creates exponential value. Network effects live in repeat interaction.

Ask yourself: What mechanic in your business makes each use more valuable than the last, for the same user?
